input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Use **`DynamicResource`** instead — it re-evaluates when the underlying resource changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when you swap the ResourceDictionary -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
```

Then swap themes at runtime by replacing the merged dictionary:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Android restarts the Activity on `uiMode` change.**
By default Android destroys and recreates the Activity when dark mode is toggled. Add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode    // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the change itself instead of restarting. You can then react to it by overriding `OnConfigurationChanged` or by listening to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` in your MAUI code to swap dictionaries automatically.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two specific .NET MAUI issues related to theming/dark mode on Android:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but colors don't update at runtime when switching themes. This is because `StaticResource` resolves once at load time and never updates. They need `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user changes dark mode in Android system settings, the app restarts completely. This is because Android by default destroys and recreates the Activity on configuration changes like `uiMode`. They need to handle this with `ConfigChanges` attribute on their `MainActivity`.


These are standard MAUI issues that come up frequently, so I can address them directly without needing to dig into their specific codebase.
